title: hieracium litwinowianum subsp. litwinowianum
intro: Hieracium litwinowianum subsp. litwinowianum, also known as Litwinow's Hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and inflammation, due to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Hieracium litwinowianum subsp. litwinowianum include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential benefits and possible interactions with other medications.
